title: Ruehssia parvifolia
intro: Ruehssia parvifolia, also known as Himalayan Ruehssia or Himalayan Ruehssia plant, is a plant species belonging to the family Annonaceae, native to the Himalayan region. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Ruehssia parvifolia include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Ruehssia parvifolia include decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, often used to treat respiratory and gastrointestinal issues.
